posts.text:
Rice paddies, fish ponds and coconut trees have all vanished beneath the waves. The land that remains has become increasingly uninhabitable.

Garry Lotulung travels to Indonesia’s northern coast, where locals are scrambling to stay above water https://www.economist.com/interactive/1843/2026/06/12/the-amphibious-villagers-of-indonesia?taid=6a2d95f53ef24f00018946eb&utm_campaign=trueanthem&utm_medium=social&utm_source=twitter
